channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: "Won't Forget": China Thanks India For Rescuing Crew From Fire-Stricken Tanker

media:content.media:description: India and China flags fly as Beijing thanks Delhi for Kerala crew rescue effort

item.description: Chinese Defence Attache You Jian thanked India's military for rescuing 12 crew members from a burning cargo ship off Kerala last year
item.content:encoded: Chinese Defence Attache You Jian thanked India's military for rescuing 12 crew members from a burning cargo ship off Kerala last year

item.title: Iran-US War News Live Updates: US Says It Bombed Iranian Op Centres, Aircraft Hangars, On 11th Night Of Strike

media:content.media:description: Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz has largely stalled

item.description: US-Iran War News Live Updates: The US State Department has issued an alert urging American citizens around the world to "exercise increased caution" over the war with Iran. The alert said that "due to heightened tensions in the Middle East.
item.content:encoded: US-Iran War News Live Updates: The US State Department has issued an alert urging American citizens around the world to "exercise increased caution" over the war with Iran. The alert said that "due to heightened tensions in the Middle East.

item.title: Trump Announces Upto 200% Tariffs On Generic Drugs, Move May Impact India

media:content.media:description: Trump said the objective of this policy is to protect the people of the United States.

item.description: India is often called the "pharmacy of the world" as it supplies generic medicines to countries across the globe. In the US, Indian generic medicines make up nearly 40 per cent by volume.
item.content:encoded: India is often called the "pharmacy of the world" as it supplies generic medicines to countries across the globe. In the US, Indian generic medicines make up nearly 40 per cent by volume.

item.title: US Military Launches 11th Consecutive Night Of Strikes On Iran

media:content.media:description: The US military said it launched an 11th night of strikes on Iran Wednesday.

item.description: US military says it's launched an 11th night of strikes on Iran as they vie for control of choked-off Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: US military says it's launched an 11th night of strikes on Iran as they vie for control of choked-off Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: US Identifies 3rd American Soldier Believed To Be Killed In Iran Attack

media:content.media:description: The confirmation brings the death count to 18 US service members killed since the war began.

item.description: The US military has confirmed that a third service member is believed to be dead in Friday's attack on a base in Jordan and identified them as Sgt. Angel S. Rampersad, 28, of Ozone Park, New York.
item.content:encoded: The US military has confirmed that a third service member is believed to be dead in Friday's attack on a base in Jordan and identified them as Sgt. Angel S. Rampersad, 28, of Ozone Park, New York.

item.title: Trump Says Imported Generic Drugs To Face 100% Tariff From August 2028
item.description: US President Donald Trump announced on Tuesday the imposition of steep tariffs on imported generic drugs from August 2028, a move he said was aimed at onshoring production of such pharmaceutical products.
item.content:encoded: US President Donald Trump announced on Tuesday the imposition of steep tariffs on imported generic drugs from August 2028, a move he said was aimed at onshoring production of such pharmaceutical products.

item.title: 40 People Kidnapped By Nigerian Gang: Report

media:content.media:description: Across the country's various, often overlapping security crises, abductions have proved lucrative.

item.description: Kidnappings have become a key moneymaking strategy for armed groups in Nigeria, including jihadists in the northeast and non-ideological gangs locally known as bandits who dominate the northwest.
item.content:encoded: Kidnappings have become a key moneymaking strategy for armed groups in Nigeria, including jihadists in the northeast and non-ideological gangs locally known as bandits who dominate the northwest.

item.title: 3 Of Hyderabad Family Die In Road Accident In US

media:content.media:description: Two people also injured in road accident in California.

item.description: Three members of a family from Hyderabad died and two others were injured in a road accident in the US state of California, according to information received by their family here on Tuesday.
item.content:encoded: Three members of a family from Hyderabad died and two others were injured in a road accident in the US state of California, according to information received by their family here on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump Vows To Help Lebanon Pursue Peace With Israel

media:content.media:description: Joseph Aoun asked Trump for political support and continued backing for the Lebanese army.

item.description: US President Donald Trump vowed Tuesday to help Lebanon "a lot" as its president, Joseph Aoun, sought Washington's backing for a fragile effort to disarm Hezbollah and forge a path to peace with Israel.
item.content:encoded: US President Donald Trump vowed Tuesday to help Lebanon "a lot" as its president, Joseph Aoun, sought Washington's backing for a fragile effort to disarm Hezbollah and forge a path to peace with Israel.

item.title: Ukraine's Zelensky Sacks Army Chief Amid War

media:content.media:description: Zelensky on Tuesday dismissed Oleksandr Syrsky, commander-in-chief of Ukraine's army.

item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said Tuesday that he was dismissing Oleksandr Syrsky, commander-in-chief of Ukraine's army, and appointing joint forces commander Mykhailo Drapaty in his place.
item.content:encoded: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said Tuesday that he was dismissing Oleksandr Syrsky, commander-in-chief of Ukraine's army, and appointing joint forces commander Mykhailo Drapaty in his place.

item.title: Pete Hegseth Estimates Iran War Has Cost $37.5 Billion So Far
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Tuesday the Iran war has cost the United States $37.5 billion so far, up from a previous estimate of nearly $29 billion in mid-May.
item.content:encoded: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Tuesday the Iran war has cost the United States $37.5 billion so far, up from a previous estimate of nearly $29 billion in mid-May.

item.title: Iran Shifted Nuclear Centrifuges To Pickaxe Mountain: Report

media:content.media:description: The facility is believed to be buried more than 300 feet beneath solid granite

item.description: According to a Wall Street Journal report citing US and Israeli officials, Israeli intelligence believes the transfer took place last autumn, months after the 12-day conflict in June 2025, when US and Israeli forces struck several of Iran's nuclear facilities.
item.content:encoded: According to a Wall Street Journal report citing US and Israeli officials, Israeli intelligence believes the transfer took place last autumn, months after the 12-day conflict in June 2025, when US and Israeli forces struck several of Iran's nuclear facilities.

item.title: "We're Not Finished": Trump Warns Of Strike On Iran's Pickaxe Mountain Nuclear Site

media:content.media:description: Trump claimed the damage already inflicted on Iran would take decades to repair

item.description: Pickaxe Mountain is located near Natanz and is believed by Western intelligence agencies to house an undeclared nuclear enrichment facility buried deep beneath the ground.
item.content:encoded: Pickaxe Mountain is located near Natanz and is believed by Western intelligence agencies to house an undeclared nuclear enrichment facility buried deep beneath the ground.

item.title: Russia Says In Touch With India Over Ukraine Deaths, But Strikes To Continue

media:content.media:description: The comments came a day after India lodged a strong diplomatic protest with Moscow

item.description: The comments came a day after India lodged a strong diplomatic protest with Moscow over the attack on the Guinea-Bissau-flagged merchant vessel MV Golden Leo, which was hit while leaving Ukraine's port of Odesa on Sunday.
item.content:encoded: The comments came a day after India lodged a strong diplomatic protest with Moscow over the attack on the Guinea-Bissau-flagged merchant vessel MV Golden Leo, which was hit while leaving Ukraine's port of Odesa on Sunday.

item.title: 11-Year-Old Died In River Thames While 'Trying To Save Friend's Shoe'
item.description: Coroner Irvine reportedly said that Kaliyah died from immersion in the river.
item.content:encoded: Coroner Irvine reportedly said that Kaliyah died from immersion in the river.

item.title: Man Falls From 12-Storey Building In Japan, Lands On Woman Pedestrian. She Died, He Survived

media:content.media:description: Fatal incident in Nagoya as woman dies after man falls from apartment building (Representative Pic).

item.description: Shinsei Taguchi fell from a 12-storey apartment building in Japan on July 19, landing on pedestrian Houra Inaba.
item.content:encoded: Shinsei Taguchi fell from a 12-storey apartment building in Japan on July 19, landing on pedestrian Houra Inaba.

item.title: Shipowner Offers Sailors 6-Fold Pay To Cross Strait Of Hormuz: Report

media:content.media:description: The proposal was circulated before the latest attacks on merchant vessels on Monday

item.description: According to a document seen by Bloomberg, a company offered seafarers an additional six months' salary if they completed a round trip through the strait to collect crude oil from Saudi Arabia or Iraq and unload it in the Gulf of Oman.
item.content:encoded: According to a document seen by Bloomberg, a company offered seafarers an additional six months' salary if they completed a round trip through the strait to collect crude oil from Saudi Arabia or Iraq and unload it in the Gulf of Oman.

item.title: Man Accused Of Killing 6 Child Welfare Workers In Germany Found Dead In Jail

media:content.media:description: No current third-party involvement in suspect's death in jail, says ministry.

item.description: The suspect, who has been partially identified in German media as 45-year-old Fatih Khan G., was found dead by jail staff in his single cell early Tuesday.
item.content:encoded: The suspect, who has been partially identified in German media as 45-year-old Fatih Khan G., was found dead by jail staff in his single cell early Tuesday.

item.title: Kuwait Says Iran Hit Several Power, Water Desalination Plants
item.description: Kuwait's ministry of electricity confirmed fires at several facilities due to repeated attacks by Iran over four days.
item.content:encoded: Kuwait's ministry of electricity confirmed fires at several facilities due to repeated attacks by Iran over four days.

item.title: 144 Refugees Dead Or Missing Since Mauritania Maritime Incidents: UN Report

media:content.media:description: UNHCR said that 17 disembarkations were recorded in Nouadhibou and Nouakchott in 2026. (Representational)

item.description: The UN refugee agency UNHCR said that overall, three rescue and disembarkation operations were conducted between July 14 and 18 in Nouadhibou, Mauritania, bringing 387 people of various nationalities safely ashore.
item.content:encoded: The UN refugee agency UNHCR said that overall, three rescue and disembarkation operations were conducted between July 14 and 18 in Nouadhibou, Mauritania, bringing 387 people of various nationalities safely ashore.
